programmes.show_description: Documentary looking at the River Thames and how it shaped London's history.
The River Clyde is one of Britain's most important rivers with an incredible history. It has been a source of wealth and a symbol of pride for the great city of Glasgow and for Scotland.
Rob Bell uncovers the extraordinary story of Tower Bridge, revealing how it was funded, how its design was revolutionary and how it is lucky to still be standing. (Ep 2)

Hector, a rescue dog from Spain, requires surgery on a painful eye condition. A cow has another eye issue - she's suffered a nasty injury and saving her eye won't be easy.
A terrier-cross puppy named Teddy torments two cats named Ernie and Molly; a re-homed cat named Olive is afraid of a standard poodle named Eddie in Wales.

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: Adventurer Ben Fogle ventures into the Appalachian mountains of Tennessee to live with the youngest 'wild' man he has visited to date.
Exploring the 'Blitzkrieg' via the extraordinary ruins that tell the story of a revolution in tactics and weapons that almost gives Hitler the chance to create his Thousand Year Reich.
Jessica finds her sleuthing skills called upon when a disembodied voice comes out of the night fog to threaten a soprano with a grisly death.
Jessica Fletcher has to set the record straight when the wrong man is arrested for the murder of a scientist who had faked his own death.
Murder mystery starring Peter Falk and Rip Torn. The shabby detective investigates when a struggling photographer is killed after winning the lottery. (1991)
Bill Nighy narrates the stories of epic train journeys. After leaving the Sri Lankan capital, Colombo, the train crosses the spectacular Nine Arch Bridge.
Learn more about an American installation that waged the Cold War, a derelict ruin on a sand dune, an industrial site lost in a forest and an abandoned tower.
In 1940, the Channel Islands comes under German occupation. Hitler becomes obsessed with keeping control of this small corner of Britain, ordering construction of fortified structures.

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: Adventurer Ben Fogle visits 47-year-old 'sea gypsy' Rick, who meanders around French Polynesia on a tiny sailing boat.
Susan explores the beautiful Cotswolds - the inspiration for romantic novelist Jilly Cooper. Susan visits Bisley, which was home to the author, Tewkesbury Abbey and Sudeley Castle.
From Cheshire cheese to ancient woodlands, canal art to bustling markets, we meet the people keeping traditions alive and celebrating the county's vibrant heritage. (Ep3)
Jane visits Kyoto, the cultural heart of Japan and former capital, where she experiences beautiful shrines and temples, atmospheric tea ceremonies and geishas dressed in exquisite kimonos. (Ep3)
Graphic drama series set inside a women's prison. Bea uses Fletch to drive a wedge between Vera and Ferguson, setting a chain of events in motion.
This episode explores work/life balance. Noel decides to make a life-changing decision - stepping back from the bakery - leading Chloe and Luke to battle for the business.
Noel and Sue mark 30 happy years of marriage with a road trip visiting the church they got married in and the site of their first business, which nearly ended their marriage!
Locals bring their collectibles for valuation, but only the best items will go under the hammer. Will either auctioneer be tempted by a teak carving, or a pair of Victorian jelly moulds? (Ep28)

Jessica sets out to prove that the death of a young movie buff was murder - as was the supposed suicide of a Hollywood director 35 years ago.
The lover of a model who helped Jessica research a book is targeted by a killer in Paris, and Jessica is faced with a slave labour scandal.

programmes.show_name: Columbo and the Murder of a Rock Star
programmes.show_description: Detective mystery starring Peter Falk and Dabney Coleman. Columbo pits his wits against a sharp defence lawyer whom he suspects has murdered his rock-star girlfriend. (1991)
Bill Nighy takes us on a beautiful train journey to the tip of Cornwall. Starting in Devon, heading towards Penzance and joining two picturesque branch lines.
Strange structures rusting away at sea. Once cutting-edge in design and construction, they now lie disused, contaminated and dangerous. But who built them? And why were they abandoned?
In a bid to dominate the Pacific, Japan builds the Yamato: the biggest battleship in the world, with the most powerful guns and the thickest armour ever mounted on a warship.

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: Ben journeys to the Pacific Northwest to live with former punk wild-child Lynx who lives, hunts, eats and sleeps in the wilderness.
Alex Polizzi rides to the rescue of the hospitality industry, in this new episode coming to the aid of a Somerset tearoom run by Mimmo and Michelle. Will Alex's plan answer their woes?

programmes.show_name: Inside the Four Seasons Park Lane
programmes.show_description: You are cordially invited to check in for a five-star stay at Four Seasons Hotel London as the iconic hotel throws open its doors to cameras for the very first time!
Budget holidaymakers and blingy big spenders go on their jollies together for the very first time. Will it be holiday heaven? Or holiday hell?
